kubernetes.tooltip: Kubernetes is an open-source system for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.
kubernetes.explanation: Why this step? Kubernetes abstracts the underlying infrastructure, allowing you to focus on application logic.

pod.tooltip: A pod is the smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes, consisting of one or more containers.
pod.explanation: Why this step? Pods provide a shared context for containers, enabling efficient resource sharing.

service.tooltip: A Service in Kubernetes is an abstraction that defines a logical set of Pods and a policy to access them.
service.explanation: Why this step? Services enable stable networking and load balancing across dynamic sets of Pods.

deployment.tooltip: A Deployment ensures that a specified number of pod replicas are running at any given time.
deployment.explanation: Why this step? Deployments provide declarative updates for Pods and ReplicaSets.
